tagline: Fire Force — spontaneous combustion turns humans into fire monsters. Company 8 fights to stop the flames and save Tokyo.
visualizationReason: Fire Force's power system revolves around Fire Breathing forms that directly counter each other in rock-paper-scissors relationships, and the Adolla Burst reveals create a branching counter-tree of elemental advantages—perfect for a counter-tree visualization where each Breathing type branches into its weaknesses and strengths.

headerFlavor.loreQuote: When you play with fire, you get burned. When I play with fire, someone else gets burned.
headerFlavor.sysWarning: // [SYS_WARN]: SPONTANEOUS COMBUSTION PROTOCOL ACTIVE — INFERNAL BREACH COUNT: UNKNOWN

introductionSummary: Fire Force is set in a world where spontaneous human combustion creates 'Infernals'—monsters made of living flame—and only special fire departments with pyrokinetic abilities can contain them. Shinra Kusakabe joins Company 8 to find the truth about his mother's death and his own mysterious Blue Fire feet. The power system is Breathing—each Fire Soldier develops a specific flame type that has defined strengths and weaknesses against other types, creating a tactical counter-tree where knowing your opponent's Breath type determines whether you live or die.

aiInsights.casual: People randomly explode into fire monsters, and only teams of firefighters who can also shoot fire can stop them. Shinra's got blue foot-fires that are somehow special, and the whole thing ties back to some ancient burning god.
aiInsights.deep: The Evangelist controls the rate of spontaneous combustion across Tokyo, creating a strategic variable no single Company can counter without coordination. Company 8 operates as the primary investigative unit, tracking Infernal conversion patterns to predict where the Evangelist will strike next.

powerSystem.name: Fire Breathing
powerSystem.subtitle: Pyrokinetic Combat Classification
powerSystem.loreDesc: Fire Soldiers manifest specific flame types through training, each with unique properties that determine combat matchups.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A typed-elemental combat system where each type has defined advantage/disadvantage relationships against other types, creating tactical rock-paper-scissors dynamics.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Arthur Boyle discovers he has a 'Sword' Breath type that's completely useless until it isn't.

powerSystem.name: Adolla Burst
powerSystem.subtitle: The Ninth Breathing / Blue Fire
powerSystem.loreDesc: A legendary ninth flame type associated with the Great Cataclysm and the Evangelist's true plan—manifesting as pure white-blue fire.
powerSystem.systemDesc: An anomaly node that operates outside the established type counter-chart, making it impossible to counter with standard Fire Breathing doctrine.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: Shinra manifests Blue Fire for the first time, revealing he's connected to the Great Cataclysm.
powerSystem.loreSubtitle: The flame that breaks the system

powerSystem.name: Infernalization
powerSystem.subtitle: Spontaneous Human Combustion
powerSystem.loreDesc: When combustion activates internally rather than through Breathing, humans become Infernals—monsters of living flame that attack indiscriminately.
powerSystem.systemDesc: A corruption event that converts human nodes into hostile fire-based entities, representing the catastrophic failure state of the combustion system.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: A familiar face turning into an Infernal, forcing Company 8 to fight someone they knew.

powerSystem.name: Great Cataclysm
powerSystem.subtitle: The Founding Fire Event
powerSystem.loreDesc: An ancient disaster 250 years ago created the current world and is directly connected to the Evangelist's plan to recreate it.
powerSystem.systemDesc: The origin-state event that established the current combustion system's parameters and created the conditions for the Evangelist's end-goal.
powerSystem.signatureMoment: The revelation that the Great Cataclysm was intentional—and is being planned again.
powerSystem.loreSubtitle: The fire that made the world

rankings.topTierLore: Company leaders who possess 2nd Generation pyrokinetic abilities, giving them additional fire manipulation beyond standard Breathing.
rankings.topTierSystem: High-output combat nodes with elemental type advantage and company-level command authority.

tiers.name: Company Captain (2nd Generation)
tiers.loreDesc: Veterans with second-generation pyrokinetic abilities and company command authority.
tiers.systemDesc: Command-layer nodes combining tactical expertise with superior fire type output.

tiers.name: Active Fire Soldier (1st Generation)
tiers.loreDesc: Standard Fire Soldiers who use the eight standard Breathing types.
tiers.systemDesc: Core response nodes operating within the typed counter-system.

tiers.name: Rookie / Provisional
tiers.loreDesc: New recruits still learning their Breath type and company procedures.
tiers.systemDesc: Entry-level nodes with unconfirmed type designation.

tiers.name: Infernal (Corrupted)
tiers.loreDesc: Former humans who underwent spontaneous combustion corruption.
tiers.systemDesc: Hostile corrupted nodes requiring immediate termination.

characters.name: Shinra Kusakabe
characters.title: Devil Foot / Blue Fire User
characters.loreBio: A rookie firefighter with Blue Fire feet who joins Company 8 to find the truth about his mother's involvement in the Great Cataclysm.
characters.systemBio: An Adolla Burst node that manifests outside the established type counter-system, making him simultaneously the system's greatest asset and greatest threat.
characters.signatureMoment: His Blue Fire activates for the first time, burning away an Infernal's flames with his foot.
characters.description: Devil Foot pyrokinetic whose smile covers trauma.

abilities.name: Adolla Burst (Blue Fire)
abilities.description: Pure white-blue fire that operates outside the standard type counter-system.

abilities.name: Foot Fire Boost
abilities.description: Can propel himself at high speed using flames expelled from his feet.

characters.name: Arthur Boyle
characters.title: Sword / Fire Swordsman
characters.loreBio: Self-described knight who fights with fire swords and unwavering conviction. Joined Company 8 because they fight for justice. His belief in his own righteousness is his primary weapon — he never questions whether his enemies deserve saving.
characters.systemBio: A specialist node with extremely narrow use cases that becomes a critical counter against specific Infernal types.
characters.signatureMoment: His supposedly useless Sword Breath cuts through a monster that was completely immune to everyone else's fire.
characters.description: Justice-obsessed knight who fights with real firepower.

abilities.name: Sword Breath
abilities.description: Creates sword-shaped fire constructs that can cut through fire-resistant materials.

abilities.name: Sword Expert Delusion
abilities.description: Believes himself to be the greatest swordsman alive, which somehow makes him more effective.

characters.name: Maki Oze
characters.title: Company 8 Vice-Captain
characters.loreBio: A disciplined vice-captain whose fire manipulation specializes in containment rather than destruction, making her essential for civilian rescue operations.
characters.systemBio: A support-optimized combat node whose fire output is tuned for protective applications rather than offensive engagement.
characters.signatureMoment: Using her flame barrier to protect trapped civilians while others fight.
characters.description: Force captain whose strength exceeds her pyrokinetic output.

abilities.name: Flame Barrier
abilities.description: Creates protective flame walls that shield civilians from Infernal attacks.

abilities.name: Fire Analysis
abilities.description: Can quickly identify the Breath type of any Infernal to guide team counter-selection.

characters.name: Takehisa Hinawa
characters.title: Flame Soldier / Lance Type
characters.loreBio: Company 8's reconnaissance specialist who uses a lance-type fire manipulation to engage Infernals at medium range.
characters.systemBio: A precision-strike node optimized for targeted Infernal weak-point engagement.
characters.signatureMoment: Identifying an Infernal's type in seconds and calling out the counter strategy before it attacks.
characters.description: Company 8s explosives specialist and team strategist. Designs custom detonation sequences that counter Infernal physiology. His analytical mind turns battles into engineering problems.

abilities.name: Lance Fire Type
abilities.description: Medium-range flame lance strikes effective against specific Infernal types.

abilities.name: Reconnaissance
abilities.description: Fast deployment and battlefield observation skills for tactical intelligence.

characters.name: Akitaru Obi
characters.title: Company 8 Captain / Former Professional Boxer
characters.loreBio: Company 8 Captain who fights with brute force and hot-blooded determination. Former professional boxer who joined the Fire Force after witnessing his brothers death in a spontaneous combustion incident. Commands Company 8 with reckless courage.
characters.systemBio: A command node with no elemental type advantage but maximum tactical experience, leading through strategy rather than power.
characters.signatureMoment: Fighting Infernals with nothing but a fire axe and human courage, inspiring everyone who watches.
characters.description: Company 8 captain whose boxing roots anchor tactical decisions.

abilities.name: Tactical Command
abilities.description: No elemental power, but unmatched ability to coordinate team Breathing combinations.

abilities.name: Ash Breath
abilities.description: Creates ash-based flames that obscure vision and smother fires.

characters.name: Tamaki Kotatsu
characters.title: Company 8 / Hot Spring User
characters.loreBio: A Fire Soldier whose hot spring ability creates steam-based attacks—unfortunately, her ability activation causes her clothes to fall off.
characters.systemBio: A steam-based combat node whose effectiveness is undermined by a critical social vulnerability mechanism.
characters.signatureMoment: Using steam to save teammates while accidentally causing chaos, then being blamed for it.
characters.description: Third Generation pyrokinetic whose flexibility lets her adapt to any combat situation. Company 8s wildcard — her improvisation forces opponents to recalibrate mid-fight.

abilities.name: Hot Spring Steam
abilities.description: Creates powerful steam bursts that can extinguish flames and disorient Infernals.

characters.name: Iris
characters.title: Company 8 / Nun
characters.loreBio: A nun who fights Infernals alongside Company 8, driven by faith that was shaken by witnessing the truth of the Great Cataclysm. Her combat effectiveness comes from Third Generation pyrokinetic ability combined with religious conviction.
characters.systemBio: A faith-based combat node whose spiritual conviction provides psychological resilience under Infernal encounter stress.
characters.signatureMoment: Continuing to fight Infernals even after discovering the Church was complicit in the Great Cataclysm cover-up.
characters.description: Third Generation pyrokinetic who fights Infernals alongside Company 8 as a nun. Her faith drives her combat choices but she has shaken trust in institutional answers.

abilities.name: Faith Healing
abilities.description: Can soothe the burning pain of Infernal corruption, buying time for treatment.

characters.name: Leonardo Burns
characters.title: Demon / Infernal Knight Commander
characters.loreBio: The Knight Commander who became the most powerful Infernal, retaining his intelligence and leading the Infernal armies against humanity.
characters.systemBio: A corruption-success node that retained command-layer functionality while operating at maximum hostile output.
characters.signatureMoment: Fighting Shinra as a fully-aware Infernal commander, demonstrating that Infernals can retain their minds.
characters.description: Third Generation whose white flames exceed normal output.

abilities.name: Infernal Command
abilities.description: Commands Infernal armies while retaining full intelligence and tactical capability.

abilities.name: Superior Fire Power
abilities.description: Dragon-level fire output that exceeds all standard Company Captains.

relationships.loreDesc: Rivals who push each other to be better, with Arthur's delusion and Shinra's seriousness creating constant friction.
relationships.systemDesc: Competitive growth edge between two nodes with complementary weaknesses.

relationships.loreDesc: Captain Obi is the father figure who gives Shinra purpose and tactical guidance.
relationships.systemDesc: Command guidance edge providing strategic framework for Shinra's raw output.

relationships.loreDesc: Maki's discipline balances Shinra's impulsiveness, making them an effective combat pair.
relationships.systemDesc: Complementary tactical pairing where Maki's protective output covers Shinra's offensive gaps.

relationships.loreDesc: Burns represents the worst-case outcome of Infernalization—and the possibility that Shinra could become the same.
relationships.systemDesc: Counter-edge representing the terminal corruption state that Shinra's Adolla Burst must prevent.

relationships.loreDesc: Iris's faith and Shinra's determination support each other through the investigation's darkest revelations.
relationships.systemDesc: Psychological resilience edge linking emotional conviction with tactical purpose.

factions.name: Company 8
factions.loreDesc: A special fire company investigating the truth behind spontaneous combustion and the Great Cataclysm.
factions.systemDesc: Investigation node pursuing the system's origin event while maintaining standard Infernal response.
factions.description: Elite Special Fire Force unit operating in Tokyo. Fights infernals caused by spontaneous combustion and investigates the phenomenon. Akitaru Obi captains the 8th. Mixed-technique composition gives structural flexibility that single-element units lack.

factions.name: Holy Sol
factions.loreDesc: The official fire defense network with eight companies each specializing in different fire types.
factions.systemDesc: Institutional response infrastructure implementing the typed counter-system.
factions.description: Religious organization whose clergy fight alongside the Special Fire Force. The Sisters of the Holy Sol — including Iris — operate as combat nuns, using faith as motivation alongside pyrokinetic ability. The organization is connected to the Evangelist but its rank-and-file members are unaware.

factions.name: Infernal Army
factions.loreDesc: An army of Infernalized humans led by Leonardo Burns, representing the catastrophic failure state of spontaneous combustion.
factions.systemDesc: Hostile node cluster representing the corruption cascade that threatens to overwhelm the human response network.
factions.description: The Evangelist's military force composed of converted Infernals. Led by Leonardo Burns, the Infernal Knights retain intelligence and serve as the primary combat arm of the Evangelist's plan to remake humanity. Operates as an opposing faction to Company 8.

factions.name: The Evangelist
factions.loreDesc: A mysterious figure planning to recreate the Great Cataclysm, whose true goals remain unclear.
factions.systemDesc: Root manipulation node controlling the cascade of Infernalization events toward a predetermined endpoint.
factions.description: The primary antagonist and architect of the Great Fire Cataclysm. Seeks to reshape humanity through spontaneous combustion. Functions as the central antagonistic force driving the conflict with the Special Fire Force.

rules.name: Breathing Types Counter Each Other
rules.subtitle: Rock-paper-scissors fire combat
rules.loreConsequence: Each of the eight fire types has a specific advantage against another and weakness against a third—choosing the wrong type matchup can be fatal.
rules.systemEquivalent: Typed counter-system where elemental advantage determines combat outcome before engagement begins.
rules.loreSubtitle: Know your type, know your fate

rules.name: Spontaneous Combustion Is Contagious
rules.subtitle: Infernal corruption spreads
rules.loreConsequence: When someone becomes an Infernal, nearby combustion events can trigger chain reactions of additional Infernalizations.
rules.systemEquivalent: Corruption propagation mechanism where one node failure can cascade into cluster-wide failure.

rules.name: Adolla Burst Ignores All Counters
rules.subtitle: The ninth flame breaks the system
rules.loreConsequence: Shinra's Blue Fire doesn't follow the type chart—it simply overpowers any fire it encounters.
rules.systemEquivalent: Anomaly node that bypasses the established counter-system's rules entirely.

rules.name: Infernalization Retains Consciousness (Rare)
rules.subtitle: Some Infernals keep their minds
rules.loreConsequence: Extremely rare cases like Leonardo Burns show that Infernals can retain their intelligence, raising questions about whether the corruption can be reversed.
rules.systemEquivalent: Corruption node that maintains high-level command functionality rather than descending to pure feral state.

counterplay.name: Third Generation vs Second Generation Ignition
counterplay.mechanic: Third Generation ignites at 25% power — faster startup but lower peak. Second Generation has higher output but slower ignition. Fast ignition can terminate the target before full power activation.
counterplay.loreDesc: Third Generation pyrokinetics sacrifice maximum output for ignition speed. This trade-off means they win against Second Generation Infernals not through power but through timing — they engage before the opponent reaches full combustion.
counterplay.systemDesc: Generation-based counter-tree: ignition speed vs peak output trade-off. Third Gen exploits the window between Second Gen ignition and full power activation. The counter only works if the Third Gen pyrokinetic engages proactively rather than reactively.

counterplay.name: Devil Foot Ignition Bypasses Infernal Defenses
counterplay.mechanic: Devil Foot produces blue fire with unknown properties. It has been shown to one-hit terminate Infernals that resist normal fire attacks. The mechanism suggests an Ignition quality rather than simple thermal output.
counterplay.loreDesc: Shinra's Devil Foot attack consistently terminates Infernals regardless of their resistance to normal fire. This is tactically significant because it means Company 8 has a reliable hard counter to even the strongest Infernals — but only through Shinra.
counterplay.systemDesc: Unique capability exception: Devil Foot operates outside the normal fire-resistance model. This makes Shinra a critical node — his removal would collapse Company 8's ability to counter high-tier Infernals. The tactical dependency on a single capability is a structural vulnerability.

counterplay.name: Physical Strength Overcomes Pyrokinetic Output
counterplay.mechanic: Maki's physical strength exceeds the output of most pyrokinetics. She can physically dominate opponents who rely on fire manipulation rather than physical combat. This makes her effective against both Infernals and rival pyrokinetics who underestimate physical capability.
counterplay.loreDesc: Maki's physical strength means she does not rely on her pyrokinetic ability in combat. This independence from fire-based combat means she is effective against opponents whose defensive tactics assume fire-based engagement.
counterplay.systemDesc: Non-fire capability node: Maki represents the physical-override path in Fire Force's combat model. Where most combatants are constrained by fire-generation capability, Maki's purely physical capability profile creates an asymmetry that fire-based defenders cannot counter.

anomalies.name: Shinra's Blue Fire Exists Outside the Type System
anomalies.ruleViolated: All fire types should follow the eight-type counter chart
anomalies.loreDesc: The Adolla Burst doesn't counter any type and isn't countered by any type—it simply overwrites.
anomalies.systemDesc: Counter-system anomaly that exposes the type chart as a constructed rather than fundamental rule.

anomalies.name: Infernalization Preserves Intelligence in Some Cases
anomalies.ruleViolated: Infernal corruption should destroy cognitive function
anomalies.loreDesc: Leonardo Burns and others retain full intelligence as Infernals, contradicting the assumption that corruption destroys the mind.
anomalies.systemDesc: Consciousness preservation edge in a system designed to produce feral corruption nodes.

anomalies.name: The Great Cataclysm Was Intentional
anomalies.loreDesc: The founding event that established the current combustion system was deliberately caused, meaning the current system was designed rather than evolved.
anomalies.systemDesc: Constructed origin event that means the system's 'natural laws' are actually a predetermined architecture.

causalEvents.name: The Great Cataclysm
causalEvents.trigger: An ancient civilization deliberately triggered a global combustion event
causalEvents.consequence: Created the current world where spontaneous combustion produces Infernals, fire types counter each other, and the Evangelist wants to do it again.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The founding catastrophe that established all current system rules.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Designed origin event that means all current system parameters are constructed, not natural.

causalEvents.name: Shinra's Mother and the Cataclysm
causalEvents.trigger: Shinra's mother was connected to the Great Cataclysm
causalEvents.consequence: Her death and her role in the Great Cataclysm become the mystery that drives Shinra's investigation.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Personal connection to the system origin event that makes Shinra's Adolla Burst not random but inherited.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Bloodline node connecting current protagonist to founding event architecture.

causalEvents.name: Shinra's First Adolla Burst
causalEvents.trigger: Shinra manifests Blue Fire against an Infernal
causalEvents.consequence: Reveals his connection to the Great Cataclysm and marks him as both the system's greatest hope and the Evangelist's primary target.
causalEvents.loreDesc: The event that transforms Shinra from a normal Fire Soldier into a system-level anomaly.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Adolla Burst activation event that establishes the protagonist as a counter-systemic node.

causalEvents.name: Company 8 Discovers the Evangelist's Plan
causalEvents.trigger: Investigation traces Infernalization back to a deliberate source
causalEvents.consequence: The fight against Infernals transforms into a fight against whoever is engineering the combustion events.
causalEvents.loreDesc: Investigation node uncovers the manipulation architecture behind apparent chaos.
causalEvents.systemDesc: Intelligence convergence event that redirects mission priority from symptom management to root cause.

systemQuestions.question: Why does spontaneous combustion create Infernals instead of pyrokinetics in some humans — and what does the Evangelist's plan reveal about why the outcome matters strategically?
systemQuestions.answer: Spontaneous combustion converts humans into Infernals when the ignition is uncontrolled. Controlled ignition at the 3rd Generation level produces pyrokinetics. The Evangelist's plan weaponizes uncontrolled combustion as a conversion mechanism — forcing humanity into a single outcome regardless of individual will. Company 8's mission is to interrupt this forced conversion and preserve the pyrokinetic differentiation.

systemQuestions.question: How does Company 8's mixed-generation composition give it structural advantages that homogeneous units lack — and why does the Evangelist's Infernal Army specifically target this mixed structure?
systemQuestions.answer: Company 8 combines 2nd Generation experience with 3rd Generation adaptability, creating tactical flexibility. Homogeneous units can be countered by type-specific responses; Company 8's mixed composition means no single counter defeats the whole unit. The Infernal Army targets this specifically because a coordinated multi-generation force disrupts the Evangelist's single-vector conversion strategy.

systemQuestions.question: Why is Shinra's relationship to the Evangelist — his biological connection — the critical variable that makes him irreplaceable to both the Evangelist's plan and Company 8's resistance?
systemQuestions.answer: Shinra carries the Evangelist's bloodline, giving him unique pyrokinetic potential and direct connection to the Evangelist's power source. This makes him simultaneously the key to the Evangelist's completion and the only one capable of stopping it. No other character occupies both sides of this structural dependency.

systemQuestions.question: Why does Leonardo Burns — as an Infernal Knight Commander — represent the failure mode of Company 8's entire mission: the possibility that Infernals can be more effective than the pyrokinetics who failed to protect the people they converted?
systemQuestions.answer: Burns converted from Knight Commander to Infernal yet retained intelligence and gained overwhelming power, proving the conversion process does not destroy capability — it amplifies it. Company 8 fights to prevent conversions, but Burns demonstrates the Infernal state is not merely a loss of humanity. His existence challenges whether the Evangelist's plan is monstrous or simply an accelerated evolution.

hero.microHook: Spontaneous combustion is turning Tokyo into Infernals — only Company 8 can stop the fire.
hero.thesis: Fire Force runs on pyrokinetic generations and Infernal conversions. Each Company is a defense node. The Evangelist reshapes humanity.
